item.title: Thousands without water after major Tamavua-i-Wai water main burst
item.description: The Water Authority of Fiji says the 375mm water main burst was discovered at around 5am while crews were carrying out water supply restoration works. WAF says the burst is located just before Delainavesi Bridge, and with no alternative supply line available, water supply is shut to allow repair works to begin.
item.content:encoded:
The Water Authority of Fiji says the 375mm water main burst was discovered at around 5am while crews were carrying out water supply restoration works.

WAF says the burst is located just before Delainavesi Bridge, and with no alternative supply line available, water supply is shut to allow repair works to begin.

WAF says it will be able to provide a definite timeframe for repairs and the restoration of water supply once the damaged section of the pipeline has been exposed.

People in higher elevation areas are already experiencing water disruptions, while those in lower-lying areas may notice low water pressure before supply is completely cut off.

WAF says people in higher areas, already affected by the Suvavou Village water main burst over the weekend, will continue to experience disruptions until the latest repairs are completed.

WAF further says schools, health facilities, and other essential services along the affected corridor will be given priority for water carting throughout the day.

People are being urged to store water if it is still available and to use it sparingly while repairs are underway.

WAF has apologised for the inconvenience and says its teams are working around the clock to restore normal water supply as quickly as possible.

item.title: Minister for Finance Esrom Immanuel to appear on fijivillage Straight Talk at 7pm Thursday
item.description: Minister for Finance, Esrom Immanuel will appear on a special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan at 7pm this Thursday. The show will focus on the 2026/2027 National Budget.
item.content:encoded:
Minister for Finance, Esrom Immanuel will appear on a special fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan at 7pm this Thursday.

The show will focus on the 2026/2027 National Budget.

You can watch the show on our facebook page, YouTube channel, TikTok and X account, fijivillage.

You can also catch the podcast, fijivillage Straight Talk With Vijay Narayan on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.

item.title: Law Society recommends abolishing FICAC and reviewing immunity provisions
item.description: Fiji Law Society President, Wylie Clarke has recommended that the Constitutional Review Commission consider whether FICAC should b

item.title: COC to consider Saneem's complaint against Mataiciwa - Rabuka
item.description: Rashika Kumar , Nilufa Buksh · 30/06/2026 Prime Minister and Chair of the Constitution Offices Commission Sitiveni Rabuka has confirmed that the secretariat has received a complaint filed by former Supervisor of Elections Mohammed Saneem against current Supervisor Ana Mataiciwa.
item.content:encoded:
Rashika Kumar , Nilufa Buksh · 30/06/2026

Prime Minister and Chair of the Constitution Offices Commission Sitiveni Rabuka has confirmed that the secretariat has received a complaint filed by former Supervisor of Elections Mohammed Saneem against current Supervisor Ana Mataiciwa.

item.title: Reduced Police budget does not reflect lack of Govt commitment - PM
item.description: Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka says the reduced budget allocation for the Fiji Police Force does not mean there is a lack of Government commitment. Responding to questions on whether the lower allocation for Police, particularly in relation to transnational crime, signals a lack of commitment from Government, Rabuka said it does not.
item.content:encoded:
Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka says the reduced budget allocation for the Fiji Police Force does not mean there is a lack of Government commitment.

Responding to questions on whether the lower allocation for Police, particularly in relation to transnational crime, signals a lack of commitment from Government, Rabuka said it does not.

He says any gaps that may arise will be covered by Fiji's partners.

Rabuka says a lot of the gaps that are going to appear, will be covered by the country's partners.

His comments come after concerns were raised about the reduced allocation for the Fiji Police Force in the 2026-2027 National Budget and its potential impact on efforts to address transnational crime.

Meanwhile, $226.5 million has been allocated to the Fiji Police Force in the 2026/2027 National Budget, compared to $240.3 million allocated in the 2025/2026 National Budget.

item.title: Fiji's maritime security boosted with new patrol boats from Japan
item.description: Fiji's maritime security will receive a significant boost with the addition of a new fleet of fast inshore patrol boats, enhancing the Fiji Navy's ability to protect the nation's maritime borders and respond to operational demands. The patrol boats were officially handed over by the Government of Japan during a ceremony attended by the Minister for Defence and Veterans Affairs , Pio Tikoduadua , a
item.content:encoded:
Fiji's maritime security will receive a significant boost with the addition of a new fleet of fast inshore patrol boats, enhancing the Fiji Navy's ability to protect the nation's maritime borders and respond to operational demands.

The patrol boats were officially handed over by the Government of Japan during a ceremony attended by the Minister for Defence and Veterans Affairs , Pio Tikoduadua , and Japan's Ambassador to Fiji, Hiroshi Tajima.

Tikoduadua says the new vessels will enhance the Fiji Navy's ability to conduct coastal surveillance, maritime patrols, search and rescue missions, and support whole-of-government operations across Fiji's extensive maritime domain.

Meanwhile, Fiji Navy expresses its sincere gratitude to the Government and people of Japan for their continued partnership and support through the Official Security Assistance framework.

The Fiji Navy says this valuable investment reinforces Fiji's ability to safeguard its sovereignty, strengthen maritime security, and ensure the safety of all who depend on the nation's waters.

item.title: 91-year-old fish seller says staying active is the secret to a long life
item.description: At 91 years old, Joji Rosinitoba Kean continues to sell fresh fish, a business he has been involved in for more than 20 years. He says staying active and maintaining a positive outlook on life have helped him remain strong.
item.content:encoded:
At 91 years old, Joji Rosinitoba Kean continues to sell fresh fish, a business he has been involved in for more than 20 years.

He says staying active and maintaining a positive outlook on life have helped him remain strong.

Kean, who is from Nakasaleka, Kadavu, says he is grateful to God for giving him the health and strength to continue working despite his age.

He says while he experiences body aches, his faith has carried him through the years.

The 91-year-old says he is also thankful for the support of his six children, who help him with his fish-selling business.

Kean says his focus is to enjoy life and remain happy, adding that no one knows when their time will come.

Reflecting on the past, he says Fiji has changed significantly over the years and believes people no longer value life the way they once did.

He says the different jobs he held when he was younger taught him the importance of hard work, time management, and appreciating life.

Kean worked for Reddy Construction in Samabula for 29 years before returning to Kadavu, where he started farming kava.

He also shared a message for young people, saying they are fortunate to have access to free education, but many do not take the opportunity seriously.

Kean says education is the key to success, while ignorance leads to failure.

item.title: Finance Minister seeks explanation from FRCS CEO regarding departure of taxpayer who is allegedly linked to the $20 million tax fraud
item.description: Minister for Finance, Esrom Immanuel says he has sought clarifications from the Fiji Revenue and Customs Service CEO, Udit Singh a few times regarding the departure of the taxpayer who is allegedly linked to the $20 million tax fraud. Immanuel says he has been asking for explanations as this is very concerning.
item.content:encoded:
Minister for Finance, Esrom Immanuel says he has sought clarifications from the Fiji Revenue and Customs Service CEO, Udit Singh a few times regarding the departure of the taxpayer who is allegedly linked to the $20 million tax fraud.

Immanuel says he has been asking for explanations as this is very concerning.

He says he has been informed that there were two letters signed off and sent to the Immigration Department - one revoking the Departure Prohibition Order followed by an immediate new order to prohibit the taxpayer from leaving the country.

The Minister says he has been assured that an investigation is also underway.

Immanuel says they are also looking at tax recovery measures for the taxpayer.

The taxpayer is a naturalized Fiji citizen and is also a Chinese national.

The Finance Minister further says the FRCS Board also discusses the large amount of taxes being owed by some people, and to ensure there is timely and appropriate action.

item.title: FRCS clarifies an immediate Departure Order was issued after revocation of previous order for man linked to alleged $20M tax fraud
item.description: Following the circulation of a letter dated 13th April this year and signed off by the Fiji Revenue and Customs Service CEO that t

item.description: The Commander of the Republic of Fiji Military Forces, Major General Jone Kalouniwai says they would gently offer that a nation wh

item.title: FHTA rejects claims tourism industry backed 5% Tourism Services Tax
item.description: The Fiji Hotel and Tourism Association says the tourism industry did not propose, endorse or broadly agree to the Government's pro
